title: Social networks, cultural pride, and historical loss among non-reservation American Indian/Alaska native emerging adults.

assets.abstract: Generations of American Indian/Alaska Native (AI/AN) populations in the United States have endured disruptions to their community networks due to policies such as forced relocation and language prohibitions. Traumas resulting from these disruptions may have a ripple effect on subsequent generations’ mental, behavioral, and physical health. Evidence shows that cultural identity and participation in traditional practices promote resilience and protect health. Culturally-tailored health interventio
